title: PostScriptum Executes Strategic Investment in SemiQon alongside €30 Million ($34.35 Million USD) Quantum Fund Commitment - Quantum Computing Report
gist: PostScriptum, the founder's office led by Silo AI co-founder Peter Sarlin, took a strategic equity stake in Finnish cryo-CMOS chipmaker SemiQon and committed a 30 million euro ($34.35M) fund to foundational quantum hardware-layer technologies. SemiQon's silicon cryo-CMOS control chips run inside the cryostat to remove bulky coaxial wiring, and their compatibility with standard high-volume CMOS foundry lines is pitched as the key to a modular scaling path from hundreds of qubits to millions.

insights.claim: SemiQon's cryo-optimized silicon integrated circuits operate directly inside the ultra-cold cryostat, positioning readout and control electronics adjacent to the qubits without thermal or magnetic interference to shrink the interconnect layer and enable a modular scaling path from hundreds of qubits to millions.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Interconnect wiring is a primary scaling wall; a cryostat-resident control chip that removes it targets a genuine chokepoint every million-qubit roadmap must solve.
insights.locator: SemiQon's hardware platform addresses one of the primary scaling bottlenecks

insights.claim: The heavy coaxial wiring assemblies traditionally required to route signals between sub-millikelvin quantum processors and room-temperature control racks are described as one of the primary bottlenecks hindering deep-tech computing.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Frames control-electronics wiring, not qubit count alone, as the gating constraint; investors should weight the control-layer supply chain accordingly.

insights.claim: SemiQon's chips are designed for native compatibility with standard high-volume CMOS manufacturing lines, mass-produced on the exact lithography equipment, silicon wafers, and testing parameters used by traditional global microchip foundries rather than custom boutique fabs.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Riding existing foundry infrastructure is a cost and scale advantage that de-risks volume manufacturing; a key differentiator if it holds versus bespoke-fab rivals.
insights.locator: A major commercial advantage of SemiQon's technology

insights.claim: SemiQon, led by co-founder and CEO Himadri Majumdar, was spun out of VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland in 2025 and manufactures on-shore at the Micronova fabrication facility in Espoo, aiming to supply control and readout subsystems to third-party quantum OEMs and system integrators globally and to serve autonomous space-tech and HPC demand.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A component-supplier model selling to multiple OEMs is a picks-and-shovels play on quantum scaling, less exposed to any single hardware architecture winning.

insights.claim: PostScriptum committed a 30 million euro ($34.35 million USD) fund dedicated explicitly to foundational hardware-layer technologies such as cryo-electronics, sub-kelvin attenuation, and cooling modules required to transition quantum computers into the million-qubit execution era.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Dedicated capital targeting the enabling hardware layer, not qubit vendors, signals where a sophisticated deep-tech investor sees the scaling value accruing.
insights.locator: Coinciding with the transaction, PostScriptum announced

insights.claim: PostScriptum, the founder's office directed by Silo AI co-founder Peter Sarlin, appointed former VTT CEO Antti Vasara (also Chair of SemiQon's board) as VP of Quantum, positioning the firm alongside incubations including the quantum-AI lab Qutwo to build a vertically integrated European hub for sovereign control electronics and reinforce Finland's supply-chain position.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: European sovereign-electronics vertical integration is the strategic thesis; Vasara's dual role as VP and SemiQon chair shows tight founder-office alignment behind the bet.
insights.locator: To drive its broader quantum portfolio
